result:
No errors. The build is clean.

## Summary

**Root cause:** `protected_call_raw` at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1459` only read the error message from the stack for `LuaStatus::ErrRun` and `LuaStatus::ErrSyntax`. For `LuaStatus::ErrErr` (and `ErrMem`/`ErrGc`/`ErrFile`), it fell into the `_ =>` arm which discarded the stack value and returned `LuaError::with_status(status)` — and `with_status(ErrErr)` produces `LuaError::Error`, whose `into_value()` returns `LuaValue::Nil`. That nil became the second return of `xpcall`, so `string.find(msg, "error")` blew up with "bad argument #1 (string expected, got nil)" in the `calls.lua` C-stack-overflow-while-handling-overflow test.

**Fix:** Changed `protected_call_raw` to read the stack value at `func` (which `set_error_obj` populated with the appropriate message — `"error in error handling"` for `ErrErr`, `memerrmsg` for `ErrMem`, etc.) for *all* error statuses except `ErrSyntax` (kept as `Syntax`) and `Yield`. That value is now wrapped as `LuaError::Runtime(err_val)`, so `into_value()` preserves the real message.

**Verified:** Isolated test of `xpcall(loop, loop)` followed by `assert(not err and string.find(msg, "error"))` now passes (previously failed with the same error as the full file). `cargo build -p lua-cli` is clean. The mega-loop will re-test the full `calls.lua`.

tool_input.command:
cat > /tmp/test_tc.lua <<'EOF'
print("testing tail calls")

function deep (n) if n>0 then return deep(n-1) else return 101 end end
print("a:", deep(30000))
EOF
./target/debug/lua-rs /tmp/test_tc.lua 2>&1 | tail -10

rm reference/lua-c/testes/_dbg_calls_tc.lua harness/impl/official/_dbg_calls_tc.combined.lua harness/impl/official/_dbg_calls_tc.out harness/impl/test_tc.lua 2>/dev/null; ls reference/lua-c/testes/_dbg_calls_tc.lua 2>&1
